description: The Second Noble Truth — the origin of suffering. Taṇhā is compulsive craving arising from contact between sense faculties and sense objects, conditioned by avijjā (ignorance). Three types: kāma-taṇhā (sensual craving), bhava-taṇhā (craving for existence), vibhava-taṇhā (craving for non-existence). Taṇhā conditions upādāna (clinging), which perpetuates the cycle of rebirth.
